identity.title: Prove or reject the bounded Rust-only YouTube challenge path

authorization.reference: Operator authorized creation of a detailed no-context STUB for this future work. This authorization does not permit product implementation while lifecycle.status is STUB and does not authorize any external JavaScript runtime.

blockers: STUB status forbids implementation until the Operator promotes and activates this packet.
WP-FF-003-executable-gate-bootstrap-v1 must be VALIDATED before the prototype package is added.
The pinned yt-dlp EJS/YouTube challenge corpus from WP-FF-004 and versioned JavaScript worker IPC/limits from WP-FF-005 must exist or be explicitly co-developed under activated packet scope.
Deno, Node.js, Bun, external QuickJS, Wasmtime, or any other external runtime is not authorized as implementation or fallback.
lifecycle.next_action: After prerequisites and explicit activation, research current pure-Rust engine candidates and pinned yt-dlp EJS implementation evidence, finalize the deterministic challenge corpus and resource/security thresholds, then promote to READY_FOR_DEV without adding an unauthorized runtime.

scope.summary: Build a prototype-only, policy-marked non-shipped Ferric-owned Rust worker around a maintained pure-Rust JavaScript engine candidate and produce measured corpus evidence for correctness, isolation, termination, memory, recycling, and performance.
in_scope: Current primary-source evaluation and minimal selection of maintained pure-Rust JavaScript engine candidates compatible with the authorized dependency boundary.
A non-shipped Rust worker prototype with fresh capability-free per-job contexts and bounded length-prefixed versioned IPC.
Pinned yt-dlp EJS/YouTube challenge corpus execution with exact pass/fail classification and engine/version/script provenance.
Deterministic host inputs, script hashes, deadlines, recursion/depth, memory, input/output, environment, filesystem, network, process, and worker-lifetime limits.
Worker cancellation acknowledgement, crash/timeout/protocol-fault handling, recycling, quarantine, cache-key correctness, and no cross-job heap state.
Machine-readable operator-ready evidence that either selects the Rust-only path or reports exact failures and residual uncertainty without bypassing the dependency boundary.
No-context manual instructions for running the corpus, inspecting reports, reproducing failures, and recovering stuck workers.
non_goals: Broad YouTube extractor implementation or full YouTube parity.
Adding any external JavaScript runtime, Python bridge, production yt-dlp invocation, Wasm runtime, or native dependency.
Treating ECMAScript standards conformance as sufficient challenge compatibility proof.
Allowing JavaScript worker access to arbitrary network, filesystem, inherited secrets, or product control.
Shipping the prototype package before a later active packet explicitly promotes an accepted path.
dependencies: WP-FF-004 pinned EJS/YouTube challenge corpus and oracle provenance
WP-FF-005 JavaScript worker IPC, event/error/cancellation contracts and limits
Explicit Operator approval before any dependency outside Rust plus FFmpeg/ffprobe

acceptance_matrix.requirement: Research compares maintained pure-Rust engine candidates using current primary source, release, security, implementation, test, and maintenance evidence; the selected minimal candidate and rejected alternatives are recorded without authorizing an external runtime.

The non-shipped worker executes every mandatory pinned EJS/challenge corpus case and reports stable case ID, engine/version, script hash, deterministic inputs, result/error, duration, peak resource observations, and exact mismatch; the passing threshold is 100 percent of mandatory deterministic cases or an Operator-resolved evidence outcome.

Tests prove fresh per-job contexts, no arbitrary network/filesystem/process capability, no inherited secrets, bounded framed IPC, bounded input/output/depth/time/memory, cache keys including every behavior-affecting input, and no durable cross-job worker heap state.

Cancellation, timeout, memory limit, worker crash, partial/oversized/malformed frame, protocol mismatch, sandbox violation, stale cache, and recycle/quarantine thresholds produce bounded typed outcomes with no orphan worker and reproducible evidence.

Source, dependency, process, and runtime scans prove no Python, yt-dlp production execution, Deno, Node.js, Bun, external QuickJS, Wasm runtime, build-time download, prebuilt binary, unapproved FFI, or native link was introduced.
Canonical architecture, PR, and triggered deep gates pass and a machine-readable verdict is PASS_RUST_ONLY_PATH or FAILED_SPIKE_REQUIRES_OPERATOR_DECISION; neither verdict claims full YouTube parity.
The no-context model manual documents corpus execution, limits, reports, failure reproduction, worker cleanup/recovery, security constraints, and the prohibition on unauthorized fallback runtimes.

blockers: Planning-only STUB; no implementation checkpoint exists.
last_checkpoint.next_action: Wait for prerequisites and explicit activation, then perform source-level candidate research and finalize the bounded corpus contract.

not_proven: Sandbox, termination, memory, recycling, or cancellation behavior
Dependency-boundary compliance of a future implementation

stub_implementation_brief.base_scope: Resolve the highest-risk Rust-only YouTube challenge dependency question through a bounded non-shipped worker and executable pinned corpus, returning proof or exact failure evidence.

implementation_readiness_gate_items: Versioned serializable source graph, identity, plugin IPC, event, error, cancellation, and process protocols
Executable phase gates with machine-readable reports

promotion_requirements: Verify current pure-Rust JavaScript engine candidates through official source, docs, releases, issue trackers, security records, benchmarks, and implementation tests; do not rely on model memory.
Inspect the pinned yt-dlp 2026.07.04 EJS/challenge implementation and WP-004 corpus provenance to ensure the spike tests actual required behavior.
Inspect WP-005 worker IPC/limits and reuse them; if unavailable, activate an explicit co-development dependency rather than inventing an incompatible protocol.
Define mandatory cases, 100-percent pass rule, resource ceilings, OS containment assumptions, timing methodology, and failed-spike operator evidence before implementation.
planned_outputs: Machine-readable per-case and aggregate verdict reports
Operator-ready failed-spike evidence when any mandatory gate cannot pass

high_roi_additions.addition: Separate the JavaScriptEngine port and corpus runner from the candidate backend.
high_roi_additions.why_high_roi: The spike already needs a worker boundary; keeping the backend narrow makes replacement cheap if the first engine fails.
high_roi_additions.gap_or_risk_closed: Avoids binding future YouTube work to an unproven engine and reduces rework after measured failures.
high_roi_additions.reuse: WP-005 worker IPC/contracts and WP-004 corpus case IDs.
high_roi_additions.verification: Run the same conformance/corpus harness through a deliberately failing test backend and the candidate backend without changing cases.

high_roi_additions.addition: Emit deterministic failure bundles with script hash, engine identity, limits, replay seed, and minimal non-secret inputs.
high_roi_additions.why_high_roi: The runner already has this metadata; packaging it makes failures reproducible across models and machines.
high_roi_additions.gap_or_risk_closed: Prevents flaky or chat-only failure diagnosis and makes an Operator dependency decision evidence-based.
high_roi_additions.reuse: WP-003 report envelope and WP-004 stable corpus IDs/normalization.
high_roi_additions.verification: A second process replays a reported failure to the same typed outcome on the pinned environment.

high_roi_additions.addition: Add worker-pool churn and isolation soak cases while lifecycle controls are being implemented.
high_roi_additions.why_high_roi: Recycle, cache, and context reset paths already exist; a bounded soak cheaply catches cross-job leakage and orphan growth.
high_roi_additions.gap_or_risk_closed: Addresses memory drift, stale compiled artifacts, leaked contexts, and non-deterministic worker accumulation.
high_roi_additions.reuse: Worker lifecycle, deterministic corpus, resource telemetry, and controlled clock.
high_roi_additions.verification: Repeated heterogeneous jobs stay within declared RSS/process/cache bounds and cross-job secret canaries never appear.

high_roi_additions.addition: Make dependency/runtime prohibition scanning a reusable xtask policy check.
high_roi_additions.why_high_roi: The spike directly touches the dependency boundary; a reusable scan protects every later JavaScript/update change.
high_roi_additions.gap_or_risk_closed: Prevents accidental external-runtime, build-download, prebuilt-binary, FFI, or native-link introduction.
high_roi_additions.reuse: WP-003 architecture rule-to-proof map, Cargo metadata, source/build scans, and negative fixtures.
high_roi_additions.verification: Fixtures introducing each prohibited runtime/dependency mechanism must fail with its stable rule ID.

existing_systems_to_reuse: WP-003 architecture/dependency policy, xtask reports, and negative-fixture system
WP-004 pinned EJS corpus, case IDs, oracle provenance, and deterministic normalization
WP-005 JavaScript worker IPC, errors, cancellation, limits, and transition traces
Design-authorized Rust executable worker and pure-Rust engine boundary
gaps_closed: Rust-only full-YouTube feasibility becomes measured rather than assumed.
Worker sandbox, bounds, failure, recycling, and quarantine behavior becomes executable.
A failed candidate produces an Operator-ready decision surface instead of an unauthorized fallback.
Later YouTube extraction work receives a stable backend seam and reproducible corpus.

risk_scenarios.risk: Standards conformance is mistaken for challenge compatibility.
risk_scenarios.failure_scenario: The engine passes generic JavaScript tests but fails yt-dlp EJS transformations or current player challenge cases.
risk_scenarios.mitigation_and_hardening: Make the pinned mandatory challenge corpus the selection gate and treat generic conformance only as supporting evidence.
risk_scenarios.verification_needed: All mandatory deterministic EJS/challenge cases pass exactly or the aggregate verdict is failed-spike evidence.

risk_scenarios.risk: In-process or persistent state leaks capabilities or data across jobs.
risk_scenarios.failure_scenario: One challenge reads prior job state, inherited secrets, filesystem/network data, or unbounded host resources.
risk_scenarios.mitigation_and_hardening: Use a separate worker, fresh capability-free contexts, restricted environment, no network/filesystem by default, content-addressed host-owned caches, recycle thresholds, and secret canaries.
risk_scenarios.verification_needed: Cross-job canary, capability-denial, environment, cache-poisoning, and recycle tests pass under repeated jobs.

risk_scenarios.risk: Timeout or cancellation leaves an orphan or wedged worker.
risk_scenarios.failure_scenario: A nonterminating script holds resources, ignores cancellation, or poisons the worker pool.
risk_scenarios.mitigation_and_hardening: Use deadline/cancel protocol plus bounded grace, process-tree termination/reaping, quarantine, and a finite worker lifecycle with residual uncertainty reported.
risk_scenarios.verification_needed: Infinite-loop, allocation-bomb, cancellation-race, hung-IPC, child-crash, and repeated-timeout cases terminate within declared bounds with no surviving worker.

risk_scenarios.risk: A candidate pulls an unapproved native/runtime/build dependency transitively.
risk_scenarios.failure_scenario: Cargo features, build scripts, downloads, prebuilt binaries, FFI, or Cargo links violate the Rust-only boundary.
risk_scenarios.mitigation_and_hardening: Inspect source/build scripts/features/transitive graph and artifacts, disable defaults, pin lockfile, and fail architecture policy on undeclared runtime/native mechanisms.
risk_scenarios.verification_needed: Cargo metadata, lockfile, source/build scan, clean-artifact smoke, and linkage/provenance inspection show only approved boundaries.

risk_scenarios.risk: Performance is reported from unequal or warm-only work.
risk_scenarios.failure_scenario: A cached persistent worker appears fast while startup, isolation, cache miss, or failure costs are hidden.
risk_scenarios.mitigation_and_hardening: Report cold/warm/recycle/cache-hit/cache-miss topologies separately after correctness and resource gates; use distributions, not a single timing.
risk_scenarios.verification_needed: Versioned manifest records exact topology, case set, cache state, sample distribution, failures, and no speed verdict before correctness.

verification_needs: Pinned mandatory EJS/YouTube challenge corpus with per-case diagnostics
Capability denial, secret isolation, cache poisoning, and cross-job leakage probes
Infinite-loop, allocation, recursion/depth, oversized-output, malformed-frame, timeout, cancellation, crash, and orphan-process probes
Cold/warm/recycle resource and timing distributions after correctness
no_context_execution_order: Read startup authority, active packet, topology, build rules, and cited design/readiness anchors.
Confirm promotion from STUB, prerequisite proof, and exact authorization boundary; external runtimes remain forbidden.
Complete current source-level research and freeze corpus/resource/security/verdict manifests.
Implement the backend-neutral worker port and minimal non-shipped candidate adapter.
Run correctness first, then sandbox/resource/cancel/recycle/fault tests, then performance measurements.
Emit PASS_RUST_ONLY_PATH or FAILED_SPIKE_REQUIRES_OPERATOR_DECISION with exact residual uncertainty; never silently add a fallback.
Update manual and packet proof; merge only after canonical gates and independent adversarial validation pass.
